meaning: n.嘲笑,讥讽,戏弄v.嘲弄,戏弄
examples: They began to jeer and insult him more than the other boys.
Do not jeer at the mistakes or misfortunes of others.
Do not jeer at the misfortunes of others.
Those who had always thought that Mr Haas is too soft will no doubt jeer.
Why should I trust people that jeer and mock their opposition in a high level debate?
